headline.value: B reaches the gut. A builds the argument.

body.value: B reaches the gut. A builds the argument.

frame2_sub.value: Slow Build → Deep Encode and Visceral Hook → Low-Effort Hold unfold across the runtime. A chord fires when two systems cross threshold at the same second and hold.

value.name: Slow Build → Deep Encode
value.rationale: System means landed in the spec'd range and the characteristic chords appeared at the right times across the runtime.

value.name: Visceral Hook → Low-Effort Hold
value.rationale: System means landed in the spec'd range and the characteristic chords appeared at the right times across the runtime.

video_a.value: Gradual attention building over the runtime, deep language and memory engagement, climax in a Reasoning Beat at 0:32. *Built for retention.*

video_b.value: Gradual attention building over the runtime, deep language and memory engagement, climax in a Reasoning Beat at 0:32. *Built for retention.*

value.name: Visceral Hit
value.formula_human: Gut reaction is high (top 30% of clip) AND cognitive effort is low (bottom 30%) — held for at least 1 second.
value.meaning: The body reacts before the mind catches up. The anterior insula — the cortex's interoceptive hub, mapping body-state signals into felt sensation — fires high while the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex stays quiet. This pattern is the cortical signature of a moment that arrives in the chest before the brain has decided whether to care. Pre-reflective. Bypasses analysis.

value.name: Learning Moment
value.formula_human: Attention and memory encoding are both high (top 30% of clip) — held for at least 1 second.
value.meaning: The cortex is encoding what will be remembered. The dorsal attention network and the left ventrolateral prefrontal cortex co-activate — sustained attention plus the subsequent-memory effect (Wagner 1998) firing together. When these two systems peak together, the brain is doing more than processing: it is committing the moment to long-term storage. The signature of content built to stick.

value.name: Reasoning Beat
value.formula_human: Cognitive effort and language depth are both high (top 30% of clip) — held for at least 2 seconds.
value.meaning: The viewer is actively interpreting. The dorsolateral prefrontal cortex applies cognitive control while the language network — Broca's and Wernicke's together — processes semantic meaning under that control. This isn't passive listening; the cortex is restructuring its mental model in real time. The signature of content that asks the viewer to think rather than feel.

value.name: Emotional Impact
value.formula_human: Self-relevance and gut reaction are both high (top 30% of clip) — held for at least 1 second.
value.meaning: The medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC) and the anterior insula co-activate — self-relevance and visceral response firing simultaneously. The cortex is saying "this matters to me, in my body." This is the exact pattern Falk and colleagues found in 2012 predicted population-level behavior change at r=0.87 in their anti-smoking ad study. The signature of content that doesn't just inform but lands.

value.name: Social Resonance
value.formula_human: Theory-of-mind and self-relevance are both high (top 30% of clip) — held for at least 1 second.
value.meaning: The right temporo-parietal junction and the medial prefrontal cortex co-activate — the cortex is modeling another mind while asking how it relates to the self. This is the cortical pattern under parasocial bonding: the moment a viewer stops watching a creator and starts feeling like they know them. The signature of content that builds relationship, not just attention.

value.name: Cold Cognitive Work
value.formula_human: Cognitive effort is high (top 30%) AND self-relevance is low (bottom 30%) — held for at least 2 seconds.
value.meaning: The dorsolateral prefrontal cortex works hard while the medial prefrontal cortex stays quiet. The cortex is processing without integrating — solving a problem the viewer doesn't yet feel is theirs. The signature of content that informs but does not yet land.

value.name: Story Integration
value.formula_human: Memory, theory-of-mind, and language are all at or above the median — held for at least 2 seconds.
value.meaning: Three systems coordinate: memory encoding what's happening, theory-of-mind tracking who's doing it, and the language network processing how it's being told. This is the cortical signature of the brain treating content as a story rather than a series of moments — the network state under sustained narrative comprehension.

context.value: The viewer's body responds at 0:18 before deciding whether to care.

strongest.value: Memory and Language systems rose together across the runtime. The two systems fired as a team — the signature of content built to stick.

weakest.value: Self-relevance and Gut systems rose together across the runtime. The two systems fired as a team — the signature of content built to stick.

anti.value: The two systems show a measurable correlation across this video's runtime. The pattern reflects how this content recruits the cortex.

strongest.value: Attention and Gut systems rose together across the runtime. The two systems fired as a team — the signature of content built to stick.

weakest.value: Memory and Language systems rose together across the runtime. The two systems fired as a team — the signature of content built to stick.

anti.value: Effort and Gut systems rose together across the runtime. The two systems fired as a team — the signature of content built to stick.
